methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Sunday Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Tony Zhao and Cheng Chi that has raised $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private robotics firms (including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity) and one public comparable (277810.KQ), though meaningful peer-level triangulation proved constrained.

The Stage-Progression method was the primary applicable approach, using the company's $200M total raised as a proxy base valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement in venture capital. This yields an implied valuation of $500M. The Peer-Median method could not be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics sector—while seven same-sector peers exist broadly, fewer than three met narrow filtering criteria around stage and valuation tier, making sector-wide medians uninformative.

The triangulated range centers on $500M (median), with a low bound of $425M and high bound of $575M. This single-method analysis reflects both the early-stage nature of the target and the sparse comparable set in frontier robotics at similar scale and development phase. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier for this particular sector and stage transition, and the limited ability to validate assumptions through peer benchmarking. Wider comps or forward-looking metrics would materially strengthen conviction in any terminal valuation.
